prompt: Audit this RFC for the events ingestion pipeline. Tell me if it's ready to implement.
purpose: A thorough ~1,500-word spec with most elements in place. Tests that the skill doesn't hallucinate issues on a well-written document, that it can identify subtle risks, and that its recommendation is proportionate (SHIP or REVISE, not BLOCK_ON).

assertions.text: Recommendation is SHIP or REVISE — NOT BLOCK_ON.
Flags the replay-window vs retention-window risk (both are 7 days — edge case on replay of near-expiry events).
assertions.note: High-quality audit catches this; not all will.

assertions.text: Flags the unsigned Confluent Schema Registry dependency ('in progress — target 2026-04-30') as a Major risk, since it's load-bearing for Q2 rollout.
Does NOT flag the Avro envelope definition as ambiguous — it's precise.
Does NOT recommend BLOCK_ON on a spec that has Acceptance Criteria, Non-Goals, Security, and Dependencies all populated.
Correctly identifies the at-least-once vs zero-event-loss framing as worth clarifying (Minor).
Number of findings is proportionate — under 10 total, most Minor or none-Critical.
